_metadata.source: State higher education agencies, verified from official .gov and .edu sources
_metadata.note: Covers major state grant programs. Some states have additional smaller programs not listed.

stateGrants.name: Cal Grant A
stateGrants.eligibility: CA residents attending UC/CSU/eligible private colleges. GPA 3.0+ required. Family income limits apply.
stateGrants.details: Covers full tuition at UC ($12,570) or CSU ($5,742). Up to $9,708 at private colleges.

stateGrants.name: Cal Grant B
stateGrants.eligibility: Low-income CA students. GPA 2.0+ required. First year: living allowance only (~$1,648). Years 2-4: tuition + living allowance.

stateGrants.name: Middle Class Scholarship
stateGrants.eligibility: CA residents at UC/CSU with family income up to $217,000. Covers gap between financial aid and cost of attendance.

stateGrants.name: Tuition Assistance Program (TAP)
stateGrants.eligibility: NY residents attending in-state colleges. Family net taxable income up to $80,000.

stateGrants.name: Excelsior Scholarship
stateGrants.eligibility: NY residents at SUNY/CUNY. Family income up to $125,000. Last-dollar award covering remaining tuition after other aid. Must live and work in NY for years equal to award duration after graduation.
stateGrants.details: Starting 2025-2026, the $5,500 cap was removed. Now covers all remaining tuition.

stateGrants.name: TEXAS Grant
stateGrants.eligibility: TX residents at public universities. Expected Family Contribution must show financial need. Up to $5,429/semester.

stateGrants.name: Florida Bright Futures - Academic Scholars (FAS)
stateGrants.eligibility: FL residents with weighted GPA 3.5+, SAT 1330+ or ACT 29+, 100 community service hours. $212.28 per credit hour at public universities.
stateGrants.details: $212.28/credit hour (2025-2026). Covers ~100% of tuition at state universities.

stateGrants.name: Florida Bright Futures - Medallion Scholars (FMS)
stateGrants.eligibility: FL residents with weighted GPA 3.0+, SAT 1210+ or ACT 25+, 75 community service hours. $159.21 per credit hour.
stateGrants.details: $159.21/credit hour (2025-2026). Covers ~75% of tuition at state universities.

stateGrants.name: HOPE Scholarship
stateGrants.eligibility: GA residents with 3.0+ GPA in core courses. Must maintain 3.0 GPA in college. Per-credit-hour award at public institutions.
stateGrants.deadline: No separate application — automatic via FAFSA + GPA
stateGrants.details: Award is per credit hour, set annually by the Georgia Student Finance Commission.

stateGrants.name: Zell Miller Scholarship
stateGrants.eligibility: GA residents with 3.7+ GPA and SAT 1200+ (or ACT 26+). Covers full tuition at public GA colleges.
stateGrants.deadline: No separate application — automatic via FAFSA + GPA

stateGrants.name: Tuition Aid Grant (TAG)
stateGrants.eligibility: NJ residents attending NJ colleges. Award varies by institution type: county colleges ($3,098), state colleges ($9,496), public research ($10,964), private ($14,404).
stateGrants.deadline: April 15 for initial applicants; June 1 for renewals

stateGrants.name: PA State Grant (PHEAA)
stateGrants.eligibility: PA residents attending approved schools (in-state or out-of-state). Based on financial need, enrollment status, and cost of attendance.
stateGrants.deadline: May 1 for first-time applicants; May 1 for renewals

stateGrants.name: Washington College Grant
stateGrants.eligibility: WA residents. Family of 4 income up to $78,500 for full award; partial aid up to $131,000. Covers tuition at public and private colleges.
stateGrants.deadline: Apply via FAFSA/WASFA as early as possible
stateGrants.details: One of the most generous state grants in the country. Covers full public university tuition for qualifying families.

stateGrants.name: College Bound Scholarship
stateGrants.eligibility: WA students who signed up in 7th/8th grade and meet income requirements. Fills gap not covered by WA College Grant.
stateGrants.deadline: Must sign up by end of 8th grade

stateGrants.name: Monetary Award Program (MAP) Grant
stateGrants.eligibility: IL residents attending approved IL colleges. Based on financial need via FAFSA. Funds limited — apply early.
stateGrants.deadline: As soon as possible after FAFSA opens (funds awarded first-come, first-served)

stateGrants.name: Ohio College Opportunity Grant (OCOG)
stateGrants.eligibility: OH residents with SAI of 3,750 or less and household income under $96,000. Attending OH public or private colleges.

stateGrants.name: 21st Century Scholars
stateGrants.eligibility: IN students who enrolled in the program by 8th grade and meet income requirements. Covers up to 100% of tuition at IN public colleges.
stateGrants.deadline: Must enroll by end of 8th grade; maintain Scholar Pledge
stateGrants.details: Covers full tuition at public colleges, partial at private. Must complete Scholar Success Program activities.

stateGrants.name: Frank O'Bannon Grant
stateGrants.eligibility: IN residents attending IN colleges. Based on FAFSA financial need.

stateGrants.name: Oregon Opportunity Grant
stateGrants.eligibility: OR residents attending OR colleges. Award varies by institution type: community college ($4,272), 4-year ($7,800).

stateGrants.name: NC Need-Based Scholarship (UNC System)
stateGrants.eligibility: NC residents at UNC system schools with demonstrated financial need. Award varies by institution.

stateGrants.name: MASSGrant
stateGrants.eligibility: MA residents attending MA public or private colleges with demonstrated financial need.

stateGrants.name: Michigan Tuition Grant
stateGrants.eligibility: MI residents attending MI private, non-profit colleges. Based on FAFSA financial need.

stateGrants.name: Virginia Tuition Assistance Grant (VTAG)
stateGrants.eligibility: VA residents attending eligible VA private, non-profit colleges full-time.

stateGrants.name: Minnesota State Grant
stateGrants.eligibility: MN residents attending MN colleges with demonstrated financial need.

stateGrants.name: Governor's Scholarship Program
stateGrants.eligibility: CT residents attending CT colleges. SAT 1200+ or top 20% of class, plus financial need.

stateGrants.name: Maryland Senatorial Scholarship
stateGrants.eligibility: MD residents attending MD colleges. Awarded by state senators. Award amounts vary by legislator ($400-$11,530).

stateGrants.name: Palmetto Fellows Scholarship
stateGrants.eligibility: SC residents attending SC colleges with SAT 1200+ (or ACT 27+) and 3.5+ GPA and top 6% of class.

stateGrants.name: LIFE Scholarship
stateGrants.eligibility: SC residents attending SC colleges with two of: 3.0+ GPA, SAT 1100+/ACT 24+, top 30% of class.

stateGrants.name: College Access Program (CAP) Grant
stateGrants.eligibility: KY residents attending KY colleges with SAI of 0 on FAFSA.

stateGrants.name: Tennessee HOPE Scholarship
stateGrants.eligibility: TN residents with ACT 21+/SAT 1060+ or 3.0+ GPA. Attending TN colleges. $1,500/semester at 4-year public.

stateGrants.name: Alabama Student Grant Program
stateGrants.eligibility: AL residents attending eligible AL private colleges half-time or more.

stateGrants.name: Colorado Student Grant
stateGrants.eligibility: CO residents attending CO public colleges with demonstrated financial need.

stateGrants.name: Arizona Leveraging Educational Assistance Partnership (AZ LEAP)
stateGrants.eligibility: AZ residents with substantial financial need attending AZ colleges.

stateGrants.name: Wisconsin Grant
stateGrants.eligibility: WI residents at UW System, WI Technical College, or tribal college with financial need.
